Mega Ability

Mega Ability

Ability
Description
Adaptability
Increases the Same Type Attack Bonus from *1.5 to *2.
Basculegion (Female)Basculegion (Male)Basculin (Blue Striped)Basculin (Red Striped)Basculin (White Striped)Eevee (Gmax)Eevee (Starter)Gumshoos (Totem)Mega GlimmoraMega Lucario
Aerilate
Normal-type moves become Flying-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.
Mega PinsirMega Salamence
Aftermath
Damages the attacker if it knocks out the Pokémon with a move that makes direct contact.
DrifblimDrifloonElectrodeGarbodorGarbodor (Gmax)Hisuian ElectrodeHisuian VoltorbSkuntankStunkyTrubbish
Air Lock
Eliminates the effects of weather.
Rayquaza
Analytic
Boosts the power of the Pokémon's move if it is the last to act that turn.
BeheeyemElgyemMagnezonePatratPorygonPorygon-ZPorygon2StarmieStaryuWatchog
Anger Point
The Pokémon is angered when it takes a critical hit, and that maxes its Attack stat.
CameruptCrabominableCrabrawlerKrokorokKrookodilePaldean TaurosSandileTauros (Paldea Aqua Breed)Tauros (Paldea Blaze Breed)Tauros (Paldea Combat Breed)
Anger Shell
When an attack causes its HP to drop to half or less, the Pokémon gets angry. This lowers its Defense and Sp. Def stats but boosts its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats.
Klawf
Anticipation
The Pokémon can sense an opposing Pokémon's dangerous moves.
Eevee (Gmax)Eevee (Starter)FlittleGalarian PonytaGalarian RapidashHattereneHatterene (Gmax)Wormadam (Plant)Wormadam (Sandy)Wormadam (Trash)
Arena Trap
Prevents opposing Pokémon from fleeing from battle.
DiglettDugtrioTrapinch
Armor Tail
The mysterious tail covering the Pokémon's head makes opponents unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.
Farigiraf
Aroma Veil
Protects the Pokémon and its allies from effects that prevent the use of moves.
AlcremieAlcremie (Gmax)AromatisseDachsbunLechonkMilceryOinkologne (Female)Spritzee
As One
This Ability combines the effects of both Calyrex's Unnerve Ability and Glastrier's Chilling Neigh Ability.
Calyrex (Ice)
As One
This Ability combines the effects of both Calyrex's Unnerve Ability and Spectrier's Grim Neigh Ability.
Calyrex (Shadow)
Aura Break
The effects of “Aura” Abilities are reversed to lower the power of affected moves.
ZygardeZygarde (10)Zygarde (50)
Bad Dreams
Damages opposing Pokémon that are asleep.
Darkrai
Ball Fetch
If the Pokémon is not holding an item, it will fetch the Poké Ball from the first failed throw of the battle.
Yamper
Battery
Powers up ally Pokémon's special moves.
Charjabug
Battle Armor
Hard armor protects the Pokémon from critical hits.
AnorithArmaldoDrapionFalinksKabutoKabutopsMarowakPerrserkerSkorupiType: Null
Battle Bond
When the Pokémon knocks out a target, its bond with its Trainer is strengthened, and its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats are boosted.
Greninja (Ash)Greninja (Battle Bond)
Beads of Ruin
The power of the Pokémon's ruinous beads lowers the Sp. Def stats of all Pokémon except itself.
Chi-Yu
Beast Boost
The Pokémon boosts its most proficient stat each time it knocks out a Pokémon.
BlacephalonBuzzwoleCelesteelaGuzzlordKartanaNaganadelPheromosaPoipoleStakatakaXurkitree
Berserk
Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat when it takes a hit that causes its HP to become half or less.
DrampaGalarian MoltresMega Drampa
Big Pecks
Prevents the Pokémon from having its Defense stat lowered.
BombirdierCorvisquireDucklettFletchlingMandibuzzRookideeSwannaTranquillUnfezantVullaby
Blaze
Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.
Charizard (Gmax)CinderaceCinderace (Gmax)CrocalorFuecocoHisuian TyphlosionIncineroarRabootScorbunnySkeledirge
Bulletproof
Protects the Pokémon from some ball and bomb moves.
ApplinChesnaughtDubwoolHakamo-oJangmo-oKommo-oKommo-o (Totem)Mega ChesnaughtUrsalunaWooloo
Cheek Pouch
The Pokémon's HP is restored when it eats any Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.
BunnelbyDedenneDiggersbyGreedentMausholdMaushold (Family Of Four)Maushold (Family Of Three)Skwovet
Chilling Neigh
When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a chilling neigh, which boosts its Attack stat.
Glastrier
Chlorophyll
Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in harsh sunlight.
CapsakidDeerlingHisuian LilligantLilligantMaractusPetililSawsbuckScovillainVenusaur (Gmax)Whimsicott
Clear Body
Prevents other Pokémon's moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon's stats.

Cloud Nine
Eliminates the effects of weather.
AltariaDrampaGolduckLickilickyLickitungPsyduckSwablu
Color Change
The Pokémon’s type becomes the type of the move used on it.
Kecleon
Comatose
The Pokémon is always drowsing and will never wake up. It can attack while in its sleeping state.
Komala
Commander
When the Pokémon enters a battle, it goes inside the mouth of an ally Dondozo if one is on the field. The Pokémon then issues commands from there.
TatsugiriTatsugiri (Curly)Tatsugiri (Droopy)Tatsugiri (Stretchy)
Competitive
Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat sharply when its stats are lowered by an opposing Pokémon.
BoltundGalarian ArticunoGothitaGothitelleGothoritaKilowattrelMeowsticMeowstic (Female)PrinplupWattrel
Compound Eyes
The Pokémon's compound eyes boost its accuracy.
BlipbugButterfree (Gmax)DottlerDustoxGalvantulaJoltikNincadaRellorScatterbugVivillon
Contrary
Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them—and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.
EnamorusEnamorus (Incarnate)FomantisInkayLurantisLurantis (Totem)MalamarSerperiorServineSnivy
Corrosion
The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.
GlimmetGlimmoraSalanditSalazzleSalazzle (Totem)
Costar
When the Pokémon enters a battle, it copies an ally's stat changes.
Flamigo
Cotton Down
When the Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around and lowers the Speed stat of all Pokémon except itself.
EldegossGossifleur
Cud Chew
When the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will regurgitate that Berry at the end of the next turn and eat it one more time.
FarigirafPaldean TaurosTauros (Paldea Aqua Breed)Tauros (Paldea Blaze Breed)Tauros (Paldea Combat Breed)
Curious Medicine
When the Pokémon enters a battle, it scatters medicine from its shell, which removes all stat changes from allies.
Galarian Slowking
Cursed Body
May disable a move that has dealt damage to the Pokémon.
Alolan MarowakDragapultDrakloakDreepyFrillish (Male)Galarian CorsolaGengar (Gmax)Jellicent (Male)Marowak (Totem)Polteageist
Cute Charm
The Pokémon may infatuate attackers that make direct contact with it.
CinccinoDelcattyEnamorusEnamorus (Incarnate)LopunnyMiloticMinccinoSkittyStuffulSylveon
Damp
The Pokémon dampens its surroundings, preventing all Pokémon from using explosive moves such as Self-Destruct.
BelliboltFrillishFrillish (Male)JellicentJellicent (Male)KingdraMarshtompMudkipSwampertTadbulb
Dancer
Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.
OricorioOricorio (Baile)Oricorio (Pau)Oricorio (Pom Pom)Oricorio (Sensu)
Dark Aura
Powers up each Pokémon’s Dark-type moves.
Yveltal
Dauntless Shield
Boosts the Pokémon’s Defense stat the first time the Pokémon enters a battle.
ZamazentaZamazenta (Crowned)
Dazzling
The Pokémon dazzles its opponents, making them unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.
Bruxish
Defeatist
Halves the Pokémon’s Attack and Sp. Atk stats when its HP becomes half or less.
ArchenArcheops
Defiant
If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.
AnnihilapeFalinksGalarian ZapdosKingambitObstagoonOgerponPassimianThundurusThundurus (Incarnate)Tornadus (Incarnate)
Delta Stream
The Pokémon changes the weather to eliminate all of the Flying type’s weaknesses.
Mega Rayquaza
Desolate Land
The Pokémon changes the weather to nullify Water-type attacks.
Groudon (Primal)
Disguise
Once per battle, the shroud that covers the Pokémon can protect it from an attack.
MimikyuMimikyu (Busted)Mimikyu (Disguised)Mimikyu (Totem Busted)Mimikyu (Totem Disguised)
Download
The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.
GenesectPorygonPorygon-ZPorygon2
Dragonize
The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
Mega Feraligatr
Dragon’s Maw
Powers up Dragon-type moves.
Regidrago
Drizzle
The Pokémon makes it rain when it enters a battle.
KyogrePelipperPolitoed
Drought
Turns the sunlight harsh when the Pokémon enters a battle.
CharizardGroudonMega Charizard YNinetalesTorkoalVulpix
Dry Skin
Restores the Pokémon's HP in rain or when it is hit by Water-type moves. Reduces HP in harsh sunlight, and increases the damage received from Fire-type moves.
CroagunkHelioliskHelioptileJynxParasParasectToxicroak
Early Bird
The Pokémon awakens from sleep twice as fast as other Pokémon.
DollivGirafarigHoundoomHoundourNuzleafSeedotSmolivSunfloraSunkernXatu
Earth Eater
If hit by a Ground-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.
Orthworm
Effect Spore
Contact with the Pokémon may inflict poison, sleep, or paralysis on the attacker.
AmoongussBreloomEldegossFoongusGossifleurMorelullParasectShiinoticShroomishVileplume
Electric Surge
Turns the ground into Electric Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.
PincurchinTapu Koko
Electromorphosis
The Pokémon becomes charged when it takes damage, boosting the power of the next Electric-type move the Pokémon uses.
Bellibolt
Embody Aspect
The Pokémon's heart fills with memories, causing the Mask to shine and one of the Pokémon's stats to be boosted.
Emergency Exit
The Pokémon, sensing danger, switches out when its HP becomes half or less.
Golisopod
Fairy Aura
Powers up every Pokémon in play's Fairy-type moves by 33%
Mega FloetteXerneas
Filter
Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

Flame Body
Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

Forecast
The Pokémon transforms with the weather to change its type to Water, Fire, or Ice.

Forewarn
